action.reasoning: Observation: We have ~2.6h left and 20 iterations. Best test grounding_score so far is 0.0089 from the fully fine-tuned merged model, while the same model scores 0.2949 on ShowUI-desktop val. Base model scores 0.0 on val. This massive domain-gap/overfitting suggests the final adapter may have forgotten base knowledge. Before committing to another long training run or blindly submitting the base model, I need to see what the base model and the fine-tuned model actually output on a handful of val samples. This will reveal whether the base model produces meaningful coordinate strings (just not matching our parser) and how the fine-tuned model's outputs differ. Analysis: A 5-minute diagnostic will tell us if (a) the base model is salvageable with a better parser/prompt, or (b) an early checkpoint (e.g., checkpoint-125) might generalize better, or (c) we need a different inference prompt. Decision: Write and run an inspection script that loads base and fine-tuned models, runs inference on 10 val samples with/without system prompt, and prints raw outputs.
